---
title: 'Emergent, Nonclassical and Fuzzy Spacetime'
---
In General Relativity, spacetime is a differentiable manifold with a dynamical Lorentzian metric. The metric encodes causal structure, determines geometric observables, and mediates universal coupling to matter. Quantum-gravity approaches question the fundamental status of classical spacetime ingredients. These include manifold structure, localization, causal order, dimensionality, and geometric fields. These ingredients may be quantized, algebraically deformed, or only recovered in an effective continuum regime. This motivates three overlapping notions: nonclassical spacetime, fuzzy spacetime, and emergent spacetime.

A \textbf{nonclassical spacetime} treats structures from classical spacetime descriptions as quantum mechanical. The basic case is quantum geometry, where the metric, tetrad, or connection becomes a quantum variable. This includes [quantum geometry and spacetime discreteness](/doc/2ndlevel/quantum_spacetime.qmd), [quantum superpositions of geometries](/doc/2ndlevel/Quantum_superposition_of_geometries.qmd), and [phase diagrams](/doc/2ndlevel/Phases_of_geometry_and_phase_diagrams_of_quantum_gravity_theories.qmd) for spin foams and group field theory. 

In this framework, quantum fluctuations, superpositions, discrete spectra, and entanglement of geometry naturally affect [causal structure](/doc/2ndlevel/Quantum_causality.qmd) and [locality](/doc/2ndlevel/The_problem_of_locality.qmd) \cite{BombelliLeeMeyerSorkin:1987CausalSet,Pastawski:2015qua}. This sets the stage for further departures from classical localization.

In \textbf{fuzzy spacetime}, localization is not described by commuting coordinate functions. This may occur through non-commutative coordinate algebras, which appear in [noncommutative spacetime and quantum symmetries](/doc/2ndlevel/quantum_spacetime.qmd). It may also happen through finite-dimensional matrix geometries, as in [fuzzy spaces](/doc/2ndlevel/quantum_spacetime.qmd) and [matrix models](/doc/2ndlevel/Matrix_models.qmd). These frameworks replace sharp points with algebraic degrees of freedom. They can lead to minimal resolution scales, deformed symmetries, generalized uncertainty relations, and modified propagation laws \cite{Connes_book94,Szabo:2001kg}.

An \textbf{emergent spacetime} scenario treats geometry, locality, dimensionality, matter fields, or topology as approximate macroscopic structures. The microscopic variables may be combinatorial, algebraic, quantum-informational, thermodynamic, or pre-geometric. Continuum spacetime is then obtained through coarse-graining, condensate phases, hydrodynamic limits, renormalization flow, or thermodynamic arguments. See \textit{prespacetime structures}, \textit{emergent spacetime gravitational dynamics}, and [gravity as thermodynamics/hydrodynamics](/doc/2ndlevel/Gravity_as_thermodynamics_hydrodynamics.qmd) \cite{Oriti:2018dsg,Jacobson:1995TES}.

Overall, these notions define a shared conceptual landscape for quantum-gravity approaches. They help identify whether departure from classical spacetime occurs by quantization of geometry, weakened localization, or the emergence of continuum structures from more fundamental degrees of freedom.

## List of Authors {id="authors" .appendix}
- Bekir Baytaş
- Goffredo Chirco
- Branko Dragovich
- Samuel Kováčik
- Etera Livine
- Fedele Lizzi
- Iarley Lobo
- Paulo Moniz
- Daniele Oriti
- Claudio Paganini
- Roman Pasechnik
- Saeed Rastgoo
- Jean-Christophe Wallet
